reason: The step posits ||jM||=||M|| by directly invoking 'GT-hyp (H1) j order-isometric, ||jM||=||M|| for every linear operator M'. But the genuine hypothesis on j is ELEMENT-isometry: ||j(x)||_D=||x||_B for x in B(H)_sa (an order-isometric Jordan embedding preserves the norm of elements). The OPERATOR-norm identity ||jM||_op=||M||_op for M:D_sa->B(H)_sa is a one-line CONSEQUENCE, not a raw hypothesis: ||jM||_op = sup_{||z||_D<=1}||j(Mz)||_D = sup_{||z||_D<=1}||Mz||_B = ||M||_op (the middle equality is element-isometry applied to Mz in B(H)_sa). GT-hyp itself flags this ('more precisely... j acting on the codomain preserves operator norm'), conceding the operator-level statement is derived. Record the one-line derivation explicitly so element-isometry (the true hypothesis) is not conflated with the operator-norm identity (a consequence). Math is correct; this is a rigor/exposition gap, not an error.
